keyPointsSummary: Colour adjectives come before nouns (a red flower) or after be-verb (the flower is red)
'symbol of' + noun expresses what a colour represents
Judiciary verbs: a judge 'announces a verdict', a lawyer 'argues a case', a witness 'testifies'
'found guilty/not guilty' is the fixed court verdict phrase

quickQuiz.hindi: लाल रंग खतरे का प्रतीक है।
quickQuiz.answer: Red colour is a symbol of danger.

quickQuiz.hindi: हरा रंग शांति का प्रतीक है।
quickQuiz.answer: Green colour is a symbol of peace.
He is wearing a yellow shirt.

quickQuiz.hindi: यह फूल नारंगी रंग का है।
quickQuiz.answer: This flower is orange in colour.

quickQuiz.hindi: बैंगनी रंग शाही माना जाता है।
उसे गुलाबी रंग बहुत पसंद है।
quickQuiz.answer: She likes pink colour a lot.

quickQuiz.hindi: भूरे रंग की मिट्टी उपजाऊ होती है।

mustRememberRules: Colour adjectives come before nouns (a red flower) or after be-verb (the flower is red)
'symbol of' + noun expresses what a colour represents
Judiciary verbs: a judge 'announces a verdict', a lawyer 'argues a case', a witness 'testifies'
'found guilty/not guilty' is the fixed court verdict phrase
